flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-3593: 'Do I need to file contempt again?' | ROW-3603: 'I'm going to be filing contempt on you' | ROW-3717: 'So do I need to file contempt' | ROW-3719: 'Ok. I'm calling the lawyer'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: Seventh through ninth instances of financial-contempt/lawyer-call threat cluster. ADD-002: Sev-1 (recurring, paper tiger trajectory). Four separate threat instances across Sep 25 - Oct 4 (10-day span), indicating elevated payment-enforcement pressure in this period. The lawyer-call threat (ROW-3719 'Ok. I'm calling the lawyer') produces a direct compliance response: ROW-3720 (Eli, Sent): 'You don't need to do that I'll have everything up to date friday.' This is the clearest on-record evidence of the compliance mechanism working — the threat produces an immediate Eli response with a payment commitment and an explicit request that Melissa not call the lawyer. ADD-005 context: Eli's compliance response confirms his threat-monitoring posture and the functional intimidation effect of the legal-threat pattern even as it becomes a 'paper tiger' subjectively.

peripheral_flags.note: ROW-3389: 'Banging your niece, that you met when you were 30 and she was getting ready t...' — 5th predation framing (RF-04+RF-15 primary per ADD-001, RF-05 peripheral aggressive-tone). ROW-3610: 'Dumbass' — RF04-ADD-002 pattern.

flagged_messages.context_notes: ROW-3720 Eli compliance response: 'I literally just saw this. You don't need to do that I'll have everything up to date friday. If I don't have to you buy then you...' — Eli immediately moves to preempt the lawyer call with a payment commitment. This response pattern confirms the threat mechanism functions even after the pattern is established. KEY FINDING for synthesis: the legal-threat compliance cycle is still operational in Oct 2023 despite the threat type being well-established since Nov 2022.
